question_text:
Find Pivot Index

Given an array of integers nums, calculate the pivot index of this array.

The pivot index is the index where the sum of all the numbers strictly to the left of the index is equal to the sum of all the numbers strictly to the right of the index.

If the index is on the left edge of the array, then the left sum is 0 because there are no elements to the left. This also applies to the right edge of the array.

Return the leftmost pivot index. If no such index exists, return -1.
input_format: A single line of space-separated integers.
output_format: A single integer representing the leftmost pivot index, or -1 if none.
explanation: We compute the total sum of the array. Then we iterate left to right, maintaining a running `left_sum`. For each index `i`, the right sum equals `total - left_sum - nums[i]`. If `left_sum == right_sum`, we found the pivot index. We return the first (leftmost) such index. This requires a single pass for the total sum and another pass for the search — O(N) time, O(1) space. The key insight is that `right_sum = total - left_sum - nums[i]` avoids recomputing the right sum from scratch.
